# Test Case Design - WindowLift

This document contains the generated high-level test cases for the **WindowLift** architecture model.

---
## Test Case: TC.001 - WLC_Init initialises configuration and state

**Given** the ECU has just powered on
**When** `WLC_Init` is called with a valid `WLC_Config_t`
**Then** the motor duty is `0`, the phase is `WLC_IDLE`, and `pinch_flag` is cleared

### Low Level Test Case Design
*(Paste the low-level test cases generated by the AI tab here)*

---
## Test Case: TC.002 - Duty is clamped to the configured maximum

**Given** the module is initialised with `max_duty = 800`
**When** `WLC_MotorSetDuty(1000)` is requested
**Then** `WLC_MotorGetDuty` returns `800` (REQ-WLC-001)

### Low Level Test Case Design
*(Paste the low-level test cases generated by the AI tab here)*

---
## Test Case: TC.003 - Anti-pinch stops the motor

**Given** anti-pinch is enabled and the measured current exceeds the threshold
**When** `WLC_Cyclic` runs and `WLC_DetectPinch` evaluates true
**Then** the phase becomes `WLC_PINCH_STOP` and the motor duty is `0` (REQ-WLC-003)

### Low Level Test Case Design
*(Paste the low-level test cases generated by the AI tab here)*

---
## Test Case: TC.004 - Hall position is scaled to range

**Given** the module is initialised
**When** `WLC_ReadHallPosition` is called
**Then** the returned value is within `0..1000` (REQ-WLC-020)

### Low Level Test Case Design
*(Paste the low-level test cases generated by the AI tab here)*

---
## Test Case: TC.005 - Cyclic task runs the control loop

**Given** the scheduler is running
**When** `WLC_Cyclic` is invoked each cycle
**Then** `WLC_UpdateStateMachine` updates `position` and evaluates pinch each cycle (REQ-WLC-040)
